answer:
The Boltzmann constant can be found from the barometric distribution formula \( n = n_0 e^{-mgh/kT} \), where the effective mass accounts for buoyancy. Using the data, the slope of \(\ln n\) vs. height gives \( mg/kT \), and with the given volume and density, the calculated value is approximately \( 1.38 \times 10^{-23} \, \text{J/K} \).  

\boxed{D}
